page: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz, providing 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 4.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core capabilities, with the Geekbench single-core score reaching 2067.0 and single-threaded gzip compression performing at 33.49 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core and memory-bound performance is limited. The stress-ng multi-core benchmark falls in the bottom 10% at 2086.47, and uncached memory read bandwidth is low at 13.13 GB/sec. This performance profile reflects the single physical core constraint and the entry-level nature of the c7i-flex tier.

As part of the c7i-flex family, this server offers a cost-conscious entry point for compute-focused tasks that do not require sustained multi-core scaling or high memory bandwidth. It is best suited for light single-threaded processing, basic web serving, utility scripting, and development environments where Intel AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets can be leveraged without the premium cost of standard c7i instances.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized server utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It is powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor, delivering 2 vCPUs via a single physical core with hyper-threading. The instance is configured with 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core execution, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2067.0 and high single-threaded compression speeds. Conversely, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are weak, reflecting the single-core hardware limitation. This server represents a budget-friendly option for compute-oriented workloads that rely on single-threaded performance, such as lightweight utility scripting, basic text processing, and development tasks utilizing Intel AVX-512 or AMX instructions.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.large is a compute-optimized x86_64 server featuring a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, 2 vCPUs, and 4.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering strong single-core performance for budget-conscious workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly, compute-optimized virtual server delivering strong single-core performance and DDR5 memory for lightweight, instruction-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 2 vCPUs and 1 physical core
4.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Strong single-core performance in Geekbench and compression tests
Support for advanced Intel AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets
No local storage and 0 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and development environments
